message:
feat(overprompting): add interactive data visualizations component

Create three interactive visualizations that enhance the article's core
concepts about overprompting and its effects on AI model performance:

1. ConstraintViz - Animated dot grid showing search space narrowing
   - 180 dots representing possible outputs from the model
   - Four constraint levels (none, light, moderate, heavy)
   - Dots progressively dim/disappear as constraints increase
   - Includes "optimal" zone highlighting to show quality degradation
   - IntersectionObserver triggers animation on scroll into view

2. QualityCurveViz - Quality vs. specificity relationship curve
   - SVG-based plot with smooth Bezier curve path
   - Demonstrates the inverted-U relationship
   - Interactive "current specificity" marker (draggable)
   - Shows quality peaking at moderate specificity
   - Gradient fill under curve for visual depth

3. PlanExecuteViz - Two-phase prompting approach toggle
   - Side-by-side comparison of Plan vs Execute phases
   - Toggle between phase views
   - Visual pipeline showing the workflow
   - Demonstrates the recommended two-step approach

Technical implementation:
- All visualizations use React hooks (useState, useEffect, useMemo)
- IntersectionObserver for scroll-triggered animations
- Module-level constants for curve dimensions
- Proper cleanup in useEffect return functions
- Explicit `type="button"` on all interactive elements
- Warm color palette: amber (#f59e0b), orange (#f97316), rose (#f43f5e)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
